name: Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership
climate: Highly diverse: tropical, arid, temperate, subarctic — spans Pacific Rim from Canada to Chile to Australia

neighbors.name: RCEP
neighbors.relevance: Overlapping Asia-Pacific membership (Japan, Australia, Malaysia, Vietnam, etc.); competing trade frameworks

neighbors.name: ASEAN
neighbors.relevance: Four ASEAN members (Brunei, Malaysia, Singapore, Vietnam) are CPTPP signatories

neighbors.name: NAFTA/USMCA
neighbors.relevance: Canada and Mexico are in both CPTPP and USMCA; US absence is notable

neighbors.name: Indo-Pacific
neighbors.relevance: CPTPP is the primary trade architecture for the Indo-Pacific region

neighbors.name: Mercosur
neighbors.relevance: Chile and Peru bridge CPTPP to South American trade networks

links.description: Seven members in both blocs; CPTPP has higher standards, RCEP has broader coverage
Four ASEAN states are CPTPP members; CPTPP rules influence ASEAN trade standards
Canada and Mexico link North American and Pacific Rim trade frameworks
Japan and Australia in both; CPTPP provides economic arm to Quad security agenda
Critical shipping lane connecting CPTPP's Southeast Asian and Pacific members
Links CPTPP Pacific members to Atlantic markets; Chile/Peru to Canada/Mexico east coast
Key shipping lane for intra-CPTPP trade between Southeast Asian and East Asian members
